description_long: This gatehouse features a robust stone structure with a wide arched passageway at its base, allowing for the movement of people or vehicles beneath. The exterior walls are constructed from light-colored stone blocks, giving it a historic and fortified appearance. The roof is a prominent, double-tiered hip design covered in dark blue shingles, topped with a decorative spire. A small, central window is set into the upper tier, while a wooden door is positioned above the archway on both visible sides. The lower section of the structure is darker, possibly indicating reinforced stone or weathered material. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and defensive architectural elements, suitable for controlling access to a walled area or estate.
description: A stone gatehouse with a double-tiered hip roof, arched passageway, and decorative spire, featuring wooden doors and a small central window.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide arched passage, wooden door above the arch, and the central window in the upper tier.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid stone wall with no visible entry, highlighting the layered roof and spire.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with an arched passage and wooden door above, but no visible window in the upper tier.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the uninterrupted stone wall and the full profile of the roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Wide arched stone passageway with a wooden door above the arch.
attribute_window_placement: Single small window centered on the upper tier above the entryway.
